content.problemStatement:
You’ve added your file to the **staging area** — now it’s time to **save that change permanently in Git’s history.**
This step is called a **commit**.

---

**What is a Commit?** \
As we learned earlier in the **Git workflow**, after adding files to the **staging area**, the next step is to **commit** them — this moves your changes from the staging area to the repository.



A **commit** is like taking a snapshot of your project at that moment in time. \
It records:
- What changes were made
- When they were made
- Who made them \
Each commit gets a **unique ID**, so you can always return to that exact version later.

---

**Commit staged files** (most common) \
Use this when you’ve already added files to the staging area using git add.
```sss
git commit -m "Your commit message"
```
